steps.title: Capture the spike-wave finding
steps.content: The passage explains that traveling waves have a height constraint because they break when steepness passes a critical threshold. Caldwell and colleagues used a circular wave tank to study spike waves, which can erupt vertically when opposing traveling waves intersect. They found that spike waves can exceed the usual traveling-wave constraint.

steps.title: Avoid overstating wave behavior
steps.content: Choice A makes a broad claim that spike waves tend to be higher than traveling waves. Choice B wrongly says spike waves exceed limits when steepness reaches the same threshold, even though other factors mediate spike-wave breaking. Choice D shifts the finding back to traveling waves. The main idea is that intersecting waves can produce unexpectedly high spike waves. The answer is C.
